page: Microsoft Azure D64as_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dav6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 64 vCPUs, derived from 32 physical cores with 2 threads per core, operating at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The CPU architecture is x86_64, supported by 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. It includes advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 (including AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, and AVX512_BF16) and cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of memory per core. There is no local storage bundled with this instance, though it is Premium Storage capable, and it does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark results indicate strong overall performance, particularly in multi-core and compute-intensive tasks. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance and Geekbench multi-core scores rank in the top tier (top 10%). Multi-core stress-ng performance, Geekbench single-core, and PassMark CPU scores are positioned in the strong top 25% tier. Memory performance is robust, with cached small-block read bandwidth and large-block write bandwidth both placing in the top 25%, while uncached large-block read bandwidth remains in the average middle 50% tier. Cryptographic and integer operations are highly efficient, as evidenced by top 25% scores in PassMark encryption and database operations. For language model workloads, the instance achieves strong top 25% performance for text generation on small and medium models, though prompt processing on larger models falls into the average middle 50% range.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D64as_v6 offers a balanced resource density, pairing a high-frequency dedicated AMD EPYC processor with a standard 1:4 memory-to-core ratio. The absence of bundled local storage and GPUs keeps the baseline hardware profile streamlined, making it a cost-effective option for workloads that rely on external Premium Storage. This virtual machine is well-suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, database operations, static web serving, and moderate machine learning inference tasks that do not require hardware acceleration.
description: The Microsoft Azure D64as_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dav6 family, powered by a dedicated 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9V74 processor. It features 64 vCPUs (32 physical cores with hyper-threading) and 256.0 GB of RAM, establishing a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio. The instance lacks bundled local storage and GPUs but is Premium Storage capable. Performance benchmarks place this server in the top 10% for single-core stress-ng and multi-core Geekbench tests, with strong top 25% marks in database operations, memory write bandwidth, and multi-threaded decompression. This hardware profile is highly efficient for multi-threaded computing, software compilation, and database workloads that utilize external storage.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64as_v6 is a general-purpose VM featuring a 64-vCPU AMD EPYC processor, 256 GB RAM, and top-tier multi-core performance, designed for database and multi-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64as_v6 is a 64-vCPU general-purpose VM with a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC CPU and 256 GB RAM, optimized for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A balanced 64-vCPU virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing and high-tier multi-core performance for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 64 vCPUs and 3.7 GHz clock speed
256.0 GB of RAM providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core memory ratio
Top-tier performance in single-core stress-ng and multi-core Geekbench benchmarks
Premium Storage capable configuration with no bundled local storage or GPUs
Optimized for database operations, software compilation, and multi-threaded applications
